lyrics:
All right
Here we are

I don't need the old excuses
Don't need "still young at heart"
Don't need every loss repainted
As some secret work of art
Some things hurt and stay that way
Some things leave and don't come back
But I can stand inside that knowledge
Without disappearing in it

There's a way to hold your head up
Without pretending nothing changed
There's a way to tell the truth
And still remain yourself
Not untouched
Not above it
Not somehow wiser for the damage
Just here, in my own name
That's enough

I don't need to win against time
That was never mine to do
I just need to stop arguing
With what the mirror says now
I have less ahead than I had once
More behind than I can hold
But I don't have to lie to stand up straight
I can do that honest

There's a way to hold your head up
Without pretending nothing changed
There's a way to tell the truth
And still remain yourself
Not untouched
Not above it
Not somehow wiser for the damage
Just here, in my own name
That's enough

Maybe dignity is smaller
Than the word once made it sound
Maybe it's just staying human
When everything gets fully seen
(fully seen)

There's a way to hold your head up
Without pretending you were spared
There's a way to keep standing there
Without insisting you're the same
Not unbroken
Not beyond it
Not immune to any of this
Just here, and not ashamed
That's enough

That's enough
Yeah
That's enough

impression.text: This track unfolds from a sparse, intimate texture into a full-bodied, earnest arrangement, creating an emotional atmosphere of reflection and quiet resolve. The energy builds dynamically from gentle, introspective verses to a soaring, cathartic chorus, giving the song a feel of hopeful anthem. The production is clean and spacious, with a prominent lead vocal sitting warmly atop a well-balanced mix of piano, layered guitars, and a solid rhythm section. A distinctive feature is the powerful final chorus, which resolves the song's tension with a full, uplifting arrangement that embodies a sense of resilient self-acceptance.

review.review_text: Rose has always been good at writing around a hard feeling instead of through it, but here he goes straight through, and it holds. The lyric refuses every comfortable lie the genre usually offers: not 'wiser for the damage,' not 'unbroken,' just 'here, and not ashamed,' which is a harder and more honest landing than most writers would dare. The song runs in E major at a pace that feels brisk for the subject matter, but the production earns it, opening spare and close-miked before the chorus fills out with layered guitars and what sounds like strings lifting the back half. That final pass through the chorus, where 'pretending nothing changed' shifts to 'pretending you were spared,' is the kind of small revision that rewards close listeners. This is Rose doing what he does best, and doing it without flinching.
anchors_used: lyric: 'not somehow wiser for the damage'
lyric shift: 'pretending nothing changed' to 'pretending you were spared'
BPM 152 noted as brisk for subject matter
